<div align="center"><h2>OPERATIVE PROFILE</h2></div>

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NOLASCOrabelo/NOLASCOrabelo/main/Cute_branding_ideas_with_cute_cat_font-removebg-preview.png" width="210" align="left" style="margin-right: 15px;">

<div align="justify">
<h3>Hey there! I'm Thiago</h3>
I'm studying and diving deeper into SOC, computer networks, and during my lunch break I learn about Docker. When I'm not studying, I'm playing MLBB. By day, I analyze security logs and hunt for threats. By night, I build home labs to simulate real-world cyber attacks.

<br><br>

<hr style="border: 1px solid #333; margin-top: 10px; margin-bottom: 10px;">

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NOLASCOrabelo/NOLASCOrabelo/main/Cyborg-removebg-preview.png" width="170" align="right" style="margin-left: 15px;">

<h3>CLASSIFIED PROJECTS (built to defend the digital realm)</h3>

📁 **SOC-LAB-V1**  
*Creation of a home monitoring environment using Elastic Stack / Wazuh.*

📁 **LOG-PARSER**  
*Python script that automated the cleanup of useless firewall logs so I don't lose my sanity.*

📁 **MALWARE-ANALYSIS**  
*Reports of suspicious artifact analysis created in controlled environment.*

<hr style="border: 1px solid #333; margin-top: 10px; margin-bottom: 10px;">
</div>

<p align="center">
  <i>"There are two types of companies: those that have been hacked, and those that don't know they've been hacked."</i>
</p>

<br>

> *"Moving in the shadows of the data layer."*  
> Every log I analyze is essentially just a small, desperate search for the needle in the digital haystack. Someday I will return to this SIEM dashboard, look at the alerts I've tuned, and wonder who let me anywhere near a SOC.
